tasks.task_description: Analyze and record test data to issue reports that use charts, graphs, or narratives.
Maintain laboratory equipment, such as microscopes, mass spectrometers, microtomes, immunostainers, tissue processors, embedding centers, and water baths.
Consult with a pathologist to determine a final diagnosis when abnormal cells are found.
Test raw materials, processes, or finished products to determine quality or quantity of materials or characteristics of a substance.
Collect blood or tissue samples from patients, observing principles of asepsis to obtain blood sample.
Perform medical research to further control or cure disease.
Cut sections of body tissues for microscopic examination, using microtomes.
Examine cells stained with dye to locate abnormalities.
Archive diagnostic material, such as histologic slides and blocks.
Conduct blood tests for transfusion purposes and perform blood counts.
Embed tissue specimens into paraffin wax blocks, or infiltrate tissue specimens with wax.
Freeze tissue specimens.
Supervise or instruct other technicians or laboratory assistants.
Set up, maintain, calibrate, clean, and test sterility of medical laboratory equipment.
Operate computerized laboratory equipment to dehydrate, decalcify, or microincinerate tissue samples.
Conduct chemical analyses of body fluids, such as blood or urine, using microscope or automatic analyzer to detect abnormalities or diseases and enter findings into computer.
Prepare standard volumetric solutions or reagents to be combined with samples, following standardized formulas or experimental procedures.
Stain tissue specimens with dyes or other chemicals to make cell details visible under microscopes.
Obtain specimens, cultivating, isolating, and identifying microorganisms for analysis.
Analyze the results of tests or experiments to ensure conformity to specifications, using special mechanical or electrical devices.
Mount tissue specimens on glass slides.
